revision.text:
{{神社| 名称= 諏訪神社| 画像= | 所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古地区|佐古山町]]字諏訪山21番| 位置= | 祭神= [[建御名方神]]| 社格= | 例祭= [[11月]]上旬 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]山内の諏訪山に位置する。
== 概要 == [[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に位置する神社で、[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[徳島城]]を築城した際に建てられ、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10～15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも桜の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた[[桜]]が咲いている。夜には境内にもぼんぼりが灯され、散策ができる。
== 祭神 == * [[建御名方神]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古地区|佐古]]三番町」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]
{{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:眉山]] [[Category:徳島市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称= 諏訪神社| 画像= | 所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古地区|佐古山町]]字諏訪山21番| 位置= | 祭神= [[建御名方神]]| 社格= | 例祭= [[11月]]上旬 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]山内の諏訪山に位置する。
== 歴史・概要 == [[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に位置する神社で、[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[徳島城]]を築城した際に建てられ、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10～15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも桜の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた[[桜]]が咲いている。夜には境内にもぼんぼりが灯され、散策ができる。周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]がある。
== 祭神 == * [[建御名方神]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古地区|佐古]]三番町」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]

{{神社| 名称= 諏訪神社| 画像= | 所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古地区|佐古山町]]字諏訪山21番| 位置= | 祭神= [[建御名方神]]| 社格= | 例祭= [[11月]]上旬 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]山内の諏訪山に位置する。
== 歴史・概要 == [[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]麓に位置する神社で、[[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[徳島城]]を築城した際に建てられ、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したと伝え、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現多祚御奈刀弥神社）から分岐したとも云われている。
[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10～15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも桜の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた[[桜]]が咲いている。夜には境内にもぼんぼりが灯され、散策ができる。周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]がある。
== 祭神 == * [[建御名方神]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古地区|佐古]]三番町」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]

revision.comment: 座標等追加、一部構成変更
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Tokushima-Suwa-Jinha.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古地区|南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }}
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]山内の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == *[[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも、もと徳島市の西方[[猪山]]に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == 13社。
== 境内 == 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。周辺には[[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]]、[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]がある。
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古地区|佐古]]三番町」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Tokushima-Suwa-Jinha.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古地区|南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }}
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]山内の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == *[[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも、もと徳島市の西方[[猪山]]に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬|馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == 13社。
== 境内 == 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。周辺には[[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]]、[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]がある。
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古地区|佐古]]三番町」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Tokushima-Suwa-Jinha.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[佐古地区|南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }}
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]北麓の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == *[[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも（[[文化 (日本)|文化]]12年（1815年）の『[[阿波志]]』）、もと徳島市の西方、猪山（城山の別称）に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。「渭津五社」<ref>もと渭津（渭山、猪山、城山とも）にあった5社で、当神社と、伊勢祠、住吉社、龍王宮、弁天社の4社。</ref>の随一と称され、藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬|馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == 13社。
== 境内 == 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。周辺には[[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]]、[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]がある。
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古地区|佐古]]三番町」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]

'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]北麓の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == *[[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも（[[文化 (日本)|文化]]12年（1815年）の『[[阿波志]]』）、もと徳島市の西方、猪山（城山の別称）に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。「渭津五社」<ref>もと渭津（渭山、猪山、城山とも）にあった5社で、当神社と、伊勢祠、住吉社、龍王宮、弁天社の4社。</ref>の随一と称され、藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬|馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == 13社

== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島町]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市伊賀町 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古三番町]]」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Tokushima-Suwa-Jinha.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }}
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]北麓の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == * [[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも（[[文化 (日本)|文化]]12年（1815年）の『[[阿波志]]』）、もと徳島市の西方、猪山（城山の別称）に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。「渭津五社」<ref>もと渭津（渭山、猪山、城山とも）にあった5社で、当神社と、伊勢祠、住吉社、龍王宮、弁天社の4社。</ref>の随一と称され、藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬|馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == * 13社
== 境内 == 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。
神社周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[椎宮八幡神社]]、[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市伊賀町 * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古三番町]]」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ]

神社周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[椎宮八幡神社]]、[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古三番町]]」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Tokushima-Suwa-Jinha.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }}
'''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]北麓の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == * [[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（1815年）の『[[阿波志]]』）、もと徳島市の西方、猪山（城山の別称）に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]裏庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。「渭津五社」<ref>もと渭津（渭山、猪山、城山とも）にあった5社で、当神社と、伊勢祠、住吉社、龍王宮、弁天社の4社。</ref>の随一と称され、藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬|馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == * 13社
== 境内 == 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。
神社周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[椎宮八幡神社]]、[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古三番町]]」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Tokushima-Suwa-Jinha.jpg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }}

== 境内 == [[ファイル:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|諏訪神社のツツジ]] 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。
ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。
神社周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[椎宮八幡神社]]、[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古三番町]]」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

{{神社 |名称= 諏訪神社 |画像= [[ファイル:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[徳島市]][[南佐古三番町]]字諏訪山21番 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神= [[建御名方神]] |社格=旧郷社 |創建=伝天正13年（1585年） |本殿=[[春日造]] |例祭= [[11月5日]] |神事= }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[徳島市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[眉山 (徳島市)|眉山]]北麓の諏訪山に位置し、「佐古のお諏訪さん」として親しまれている。
== 祭神 == * [[建御名方神]]
== 歴史 == [[天正]]13年（[[1585年]]）に[[徳島藩]]主[[蜂須賀家政]]が[[豊臣秀吉]]の命により[[渭津城|渭津城（徳島城）]]を築城した際に、城の[[鬼門]]守護の神として創祀されたといい、[[徳島城|城山]]の北東麓にあった諏訪明神社を現在地に移したとも（[[文化 (元号)|文化]]12年（1815年）の『[[阿波志]]』）、もと徳島市の西方、猪山（城山の別称）に鎮座していたとも、また一説には現[[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄の諏訪社（現[[多祁御奈刀弥神社]]）から[[分祀]]されたともいわれている。「渭津五社」<ref>もと渭津（渭山、猪山、城山とも）にあった5社で、当神社と、伊勢祠、住吉社、龍王宮、弁天社の4社。</ref>の随一と称され、藩政時代を通じて蜂須賀家の崇敬篤く、社殿の修理造営等は全て藩費でまかない、[[慶安|慶安年間]]（17世紀中頃）には[[社領]]を寄進し秋の祭礼における[[馬|馬匹]]の奉献を恒例とするようになった。
[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）5月に[[郷社]]に列した。
== 末社 == * 13社
== 境内 == [[ファイル:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|諏訪神社のツツジ]] 眉山山麓に位置する境内は[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られており、2000[[平方メートル]]の敷地に樹齢10年から15年のツツジが約100本植わっている。
ツツジの他にも[[桜]]の名所としても知られており、神社へと続く石段を囲んで大きく枝を広げた桜が咲いている。夜には境内にも[[雪洞]]が灯され、散策ができる。
神社周辺には[[三島神社 (徳島市)|三島神社]]、[[椎宮八幡神社]]、[[清水寺 (徳島市)|清水寺]]がある。
<gallery> ファイル:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|境内から見た景色。 </gallery>
== 市中五社 == * [[春日神社 (徳島市)|春日神社]] - 徳島市[[眉山町]]大滝山 * [[四所神社 (徳島市)|四所神社]] - 徳島市[[福島 (徳島市)|福島]] * [[八幡神社 (徳島市)|八幡神社]] - 徳島市[[伊賀町 (徳島市)|伊賀町]] * [[金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|金刀比羅神社]] - 徳島市[[勢見町]]
== 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島駅]]より[[徳島市営バス]]上鮎喰線「[[佐古三番町]]」下車より徒歩3分。 * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[佐古駅]]より徒歩約12分。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html 諏訪の桜] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html 諏訪神社のつつじ] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すわしんしや}} [[Category:徳島市の神社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:眉山]]

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as Tokushima Castle, under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as Tokushima Castle, under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].

Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.

<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as Tokushima Castle, under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as Tokushima Castle, under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

revision.text:
== Suwa Shrine == {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] | Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | Former shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | Founded = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|ja|11月5日}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城}} under the order of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunshi|ja|分祀}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed at {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as [[:en:Tokushima_Castle|Tokushima Castle]], under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

[[Category:Tokushima City Shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Mt. Bizan]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]]

== Suwa Shrine == {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] | Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | Former shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | Founded = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|ja|11月5日}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城}} under the order of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunshi|ja|分祀}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed at {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as [[:en:Tokushima_Castle|Tokushima Castle]], under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

revision.text:
== Suwa Shrine == {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] | Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | Former shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | Founded = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|ja|11月5日}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城}} under the order of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunshi|ja|分祀}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}}

== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |Location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |Annual Festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{Location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed at {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as [[:en:Tokushima_Castle|Tokushima Castle]], under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed at {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as [[:en:Tokushima_Castle|Tokushima Castle]], under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]

== Suwa Shrine == {{Infobox Shinto shrine | Name = Suwa Shrine | image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | Former shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecrated = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|ja|11月5日}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城}} under the order of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunshi|ja|分祀}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed at {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as [[:en:Tokushima_Castle|Tokushima Castle]], under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]
{{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
== Suwa Shrine == {{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] | location = [[Tokushima Prefecture]], [[Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} | Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | Former shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|ja|11月5日}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Tokushima City]], [[Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mt. Bizan|ja|眉山 (徳島市)}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城}} under the order of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunshi|ja|分祀}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg|250px]] |location = [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima]], [[Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Minamisako Sanbancho|ja|南佐古三番町}}, Suwayama 21 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|04|32.5|N|134|32|07.9|E|type:landmark_region:JP-36|display=inline,title}} |Main Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}} |Classification = Former Gōsha Shrine |Foundation = Said to be in Tenshō 13 (1585) |Main Hall = [[Kasuga-zukuri]] |festival = [[November 5]] |Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Tokushima City|ja|徳島市}}, [[Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. It is situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, on Suwayama, and is affectionately known as "Sako no O-Suwa-san."
== Main Deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata-no-Kami|ja|建御名方神}}
== History == The shrine was founded in 1585 (Tenshō 13), when [[Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of the [[Tokushima Domain]], constructed at {{ill|Witsujō|ja|渭津城}}, later known as [[:en:Tokushima_Castle|Tokushima Castle]], under orders from [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It was enshrined to guard the castle's [[Kimon|northeastern direction]] (considered unlucky). Some accounts suggest that the Suwa Shrine originally located at the northeastern base of Mount Jōzan (Tokushima Castle) was moved to the current location, as documented in the *Awa-shi* (1815). Other theories propose that the shrine was initially located on Inoyama (another name for Mount Jōzan) or was a branch of the {{ill|Takeminakata Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社}} in present-day [[Ishii Town]] of [[Nishi District, Tokushima|Nishi District]].
The shrine was the foremost of the "Witsu Five Shrines,"<ref>The five shrines of Witsu were Suwa Shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref> and it enjoyed the patronage of the Hachisuka clan throughout the Edo period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine were entirely funded by the domain, and by the mid-17th century (Keian era), land was granted to the shrine, and the autumn festival included offerings of horses.
In May 1870 (Meiji 3), the shrine was classified as a Gōsha (village shrine).
== Subsidiary Shrines == * 13 auxiliary shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]] Located on the slopes of {{ill|Mount Bizan|ja|眉山}}, the shrine grounds are renowned for their azaleas. Approximately 100 azaleas, aged between 10 and 15 years, are planted over an area of 2,000 square meters.
The site is also famous for cherry blossoms, with large sakura trees lining the stone steps leading to the shrine. In the evening, lanterns light up the precincts, creating a scenic pathway for visitors.
Nearby attractions include {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)}}, {{ill|Shiimiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社}}, and {{ill|Shimizu Temple (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== Tokushima City Central Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City, Bizan)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市眉山町)}} - Located in {{ill|Bizan Town|ja|眉山町}}, Tokushima City * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fukushima, Tokushima City|ja|福島 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Iga Town, Tokushima City|ja|伊賀町 (徳島市)}} * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)}} - Located in {{ill|Semimachi|ja|勢見町}}
== Access == * About a 3-minute walk from the {{ill|Sako Sanbancho|ja|佐古三番町}} stop on the Tokushima City Bus Upper Awaikui Line. * Approximately a 12-minute walk from [[JR Sako Station]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Azaleas at Suwa Shrine]
