{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|qid=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|qq=}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|qq=}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|qid=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminatomi-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Shiinomiya hachiman-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminatomi-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|qid=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|qid=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|qq=|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|qq=|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]

The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]] | Annual festival = [[:en:November 5|November 5]] | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of [[:en:Mount Bizan|Mt. Bizan]] on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]]
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in [[:en:1585|1585]] (Tenshō 13) when [[:en:Hachisuka Iemasa|Hachisuka Iemasa]], the lord of [[:en:Tokushima Domain|Tokushima Domain]], built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]]. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was [[:en:Bunrei|Branch enshrined]] from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In [[:en:1870|1870]] (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Tokushima Station|Tokushima Station]] → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * [[:en:Shikoku Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Sako Station|Sako Station]] → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:諏訪神社 (徳島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11631927]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|en|November 5|WD=Q2993|lt=November 5}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|en|Mount Bizan|WD=Q8189109|lt=Mt. Bizan}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in {{ill|1585|en|1585|WD=Q6736|lt=1585}} (Tenshō 13) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|en|Tokushima Domain|WD=Q3034557|lt=Tokushima Domain}}, built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Branch enshrined}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}} {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sako Station|en|Sako Station|WD=Q7403047|lt=Sako Station}} → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==

{{Infobox religious building | name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima.jpeg | location = {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], [[Minami Sako Sanbancho]] Suwayama 21 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.075694444444444|N|134.5355277777778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} | Former shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha]] (郷社) | consecration_year = Tradition says 1585 (Tenshō 13) | Main hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = {{ill|November 5|en|November 5|WD=Q2993|lt=November 5}} | Map = {{location map|Japan Tokushima Prefecture|label=Suwa Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | location = {{ill|Tokushima|WD=Q207309}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (すわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Tokushima City|Tokushima City]], {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}, Japan. Situated on the northern foothills of {{ill|Mount Bizan|en|Mount Bizan|WD=Q8189109|lt=Mt. Bizan}} on Suwayama, it is affectionately called "Sako's Suwa-san."
== deity == * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}
== History == The shrine was reportedly founded in {{ill|1585|en|1585|WD=Q6736|lt=1585}} (Tenshō 13) when {{ill|Hachisuka Iemasa|en|Hachisuka Iemasa|WD=Q1073557|lt=Hachisuka Iemasa}}, the lord of {{ill|Tokushima Domain|en|Tokushima Domain|WD=Q3034557|lt=Tokushima Domain}}, built {{ill|Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja|渭津城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watsu Castle (Tokushima Castle)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳島城|1=Tokushima Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8011246}} under the order of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. It is said to have been established to protect the [[kimon|demon gate]] of the castle. Another account states that it was relocated from Suwa Myōjinsha, originally at the northeastern foothills of Tokushima Castle (described in the ''Awa-shi'' of 1815), or that it was previously enshrined in {{ill|Inoyama (猪山)|ja|猪山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoyama (猪山)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another name for Tokushima Castle. Alternatively, some say it was {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Branch enshrined}} from Suwa Shrine in present-day [[Ishii Town]], [[Myōzai District]], now known as {{ill|Take Minatomiya Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Minatomiya Shrine|1=Takeminatomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759404}}.
As the foremost of the "Watsu Five Shrines"<ref>"Watsu Five Shrines" refers to the five shrines originally located on Mt. Watsu (alternatively Inoyama or Tokushima Castle). They include this shrine, Ise Shrine, Sumiyoshi Shrine, Ryūō Shrine, and Benzaiten Shrine.</ref>, Suwa Shrine enjoyed strong patronage from the Hachisuka family throughout the Tokugawa period. Repairs and reconstructions of the shrine buildings were carried out using domain funds, and during the [[Keian period]] (1648–1652), the domain granted shrine lands and established the tradition of offering horses during the autumn festival.
In {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} (Meiji 3), the shrine was designated as a [[Gōsha]].
== Sub-shrines == * 13 sub-shrines
== Grounds == [[File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima2.jpeg|thumb|240px|right|The azaleas of Suwa Shrine]] Located at the base of Mt. Bizan, the shrine grounds are known for their azaleas, with approximately 100 bushes planted over a 2,000 square meter area, ranging in age from 10 to 15 years.
The shrine is also a well-known spot for cherry blossoms, with large trees flanking the stone steps leading to the shrine. At night, the grounds are illuminated by [[snow lanterns]] (bonbori), creating a serene atmosphere for visitors.
Nearby are {{ill|Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|三島神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Shrine (Tokushima City)|1=Mishima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355277}}, {{ill|Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|椎宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Shiinomiya Hachiman Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11540834}}, and {{ill|Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|ja|清水寺 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimizu-dera (Tokushima City)|1=Seisui-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560918}}.
<gallery> File:Suwa Jinjya Tokushima3.jpeg|View from the shrine grounds. </gallery>
== City’s Five Shrines == * {{ill|Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|春日神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located on Mt. Ōtaki in [[Bizan Town]], Tokushima City. * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|四所神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Fukushima, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|八幡神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Iga Town, Tokushima City]]. * {{ill|Konpira Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|金刀比羅神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} - Located in [[Semimachi, Tokushima City]].
== Access == * {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}} {{ill|Tokushima Station|en|Tokushima Station|WD=Q860775|lt=Tokushima Station}} → [[Tokushima City Bus]] (Kami-Ayukui Line) to "Sako Sanbancho" stop, 3-minute walk. * {{ill|Shikoku Railway Company|en|Shikoku Railway Company|WD=Q496531|lt=JR}} {{ill|Sako Station|en|Sako Station|WD=Q7403047|lt=Sako Station}} → Approximately 12 minutes on foot.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001467.html Suwa Cherry Blossoms] * [http://www.awanavi.jp/article/0001469.html Suwa Shrine Azaleas]
